title: 2. Installer and Runtimes
description: What the graphical installer sets up, why MuseTalk and PocketTTS are isolated, and what optional external tools still matter.

steps.title: What the Installer Creates
steps.body: The installer creates the main Neural Companion runtime and, when selected, separate isolated runtimes for MuseTalk and PocketTTS. Isolation keeps incompatible dependency stacks from colliding.

steps.title: Main Runtime
steps.body: The main runtime contains the app, PySide UI, chat integration, Chatterbox TTS, addon framework, and ordinary core dependencies.

steps.title: MuseTalk Runtime
steps.body: MuseTalk has its own runtime because talking-head inference depends on an older CUDA/PyTorch stack. Install it only when you want MuseTalk avatars.

steps.title: PocketTTS Runtime
steps.body: PocketTTS is isolated as well. Voice cloning may require accepting Hugging Face model terms and logging in through the PocketTTS runtime. Use the TTS backend tab circle to make PocketTTS active after it is installed.

steps.title: External Tools
steps.body: FFmpeg should be on PATH. LM Studio should be installed and serving a model before you initialize chat. VSeeFace is optional if you use an external VSeeFace avatar workflow.

steps.title: Avatar Packs Are Separate
steps.body: MuseTalk avatar packs are distributed separately from the app. Import or unpack them into avatar_packs before selecting them in the MuseTalk avatar-pack control.
